summary.intent: Implement the framework-agnostic RangeSlider agnostic core (#251) in ars-components following TDD, with full snapshot/spec-conformance/proptest/mutation coverage, plus spec consistency fixes, stopping short of commit/push without explicit approval.
summary.outcome: The RangeSlider core module was fully implemented with state machine, API attrs, callbacks, pointer geometry, controlled/uncontrolled sync, per-thumb disabled, min-gap, clamp/swap drag, and 21 insta snapshots. Coverage reached 96% lines, all mutation survivors were either caught by added tests or documented as equivalent. A non-draft PR was committed and pushed with Codex co-author trailer.

repo: Component modules follow a strict pattern: State/ThumbIndex/Event/Context/Props/Messages/Effect/Machine/Part/Api types, with manual ComponentPart impl required when Part variants contain f64 fields (to use to_bits for hash/eq).
cargo-mutants is run per-file with `-- <test_filter>` to scope the test runner; `--iterate` retests only surviving/changed mutants rather than rebuilding the full set.
Equivalent mutation boundary skips go in .cargo/mutants.toml at the repo root, not inline; the format uses line-agnostic pattern matching.

`cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject` cannot be safely filtered by test name - it treats all unmatched snapshot files across the crate as orphans; only the unfiltered package-level form is valid as a gate.
The spec directory has three layers: spec/components (agnostic core), spec/leptos-components, and spec/dioxus-components; all three must be updated together when the public API changes.
Pre-existing data_display meter/progress/stat tests have a broken aria-valuetext percent-format assertion that causes the whole-crate insta orphan check and cargo-mutants baseline to fail.
The GitHub project board item is updated via `gh api graphql` + `gh project item-edit` using the project/field/option IDs obtained from a GraphQL query before implementation starts.

code.finding: PointerUp match arm originally used `let _ = thumb` to suppress an unused binding. The correct form is `ctx.dragging_thumb?;` — it keeps the active-drag guard explicit without implying thumb identity matters for the resulting state transition, which uses focused_thumb instead.

set_values_plan originally carried a drag-only parameter that no caller used, producing a dead code branch that generated unmutatable survivors. Removing the parameter eliminated those spurious mutation misses.
Two normalize_values equality-boundary mutants (>= vs > and <= vs <) are documented as equivalent skips because both sides of the boundary produce the same normalized output.

workflow: Running `cargo mutants --iterate` after adding hardening tests is significantly faster than a cold run because it retests only the previously surviving/changed mutants.
When cargo-mutants baseline fails due to unrelated pre-existing failures, use `-- <focused_test_filter>` to scope the test runner to the new module only, keeping the gate meaningful.

Mutation survivors cluster predictably: API event-dispatch methods, prop-diff detection, equality-boundary helpers, and geometry guards are the usual weak spots for a new component — targeting tests at those clusters clears most survivors in one pass.
Coverage via `cargo llvm-cov --text` prints the full crate; the useful signal is from `cargo llvm-cov report --summary-only` filtered to the target file path.
Post-implementation audit should be done in three phases: (1) spec/implementation drift, (2) two 'anything else missing?' rounds checking manifest, adapter specs, and wiring, (3) coverage review.

friction: Pre-existing data_display aria-valuetext percent-format failures blocked both the cargo-mutants unmutated baseline and the whole-crate `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` gate, requiring workarounds (scoped test filter for mutants; removing generated .snap.new files for insta).
Mutation testing ran for a very large number of rounds (355 mutants, multiple --iterate passes) and required many interactive wait cycles due to long build+test times per mutant.

`cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject -- range_slider` silently treats all non-matched crate snapshots as orphans, making it unusable as a filtered gate; this cost time to diagnose.

The RangeSlider spec contained stale code sketches (KeyboardEventData API, old callback name on_change, min_gap vs min_steps_between) that had to be discovered and corrected during the post-implementation audit rather than being caught before implementation started.
The controlled-prop resnap gap (resync needed when bounds changed without the controlled value changing) was only discovered through failing unit tests rather than being specified in the spec.
open_items: Pre-existing data_display meter/progress/stat aria-valuetext percent-format test failures are unrelated to this PR but block repo-wide insta orphan checks and should be fixed separately.
Leptos and Dioxus adapter components for RangeSlider have not been implemented — only the agnostic core and adapter specs were updated in this task.
The proptest property scenarios are registered with `#[ignore]` and are not run in CI; they need to be un-ignored once the harness supports them.

Adapter spec sketches in spec/leptos-components and spec/dioxus-components were updated to remove API drift but still contain placeholder wording that will need revision when adapter implementations are written.
